purpose: Codex-owned external memory context surface inside the wiki, separate from claude-memory and hermes-current.
single_writer: Codex writes here unless user authorizes another runtime to import or mirror.

directories.context_packets: Durable context extracts and user-feedback preservation packets.
directories.doctrine_candidates: Candidate operating rules or doctrine awaiting admission.
directories.indexes: Machine-readable indexes for lookup and routing.

rules: Preserve user ideas separately from LLM suggestions.
Preserve good LLM outputs with provenance and status.
Do not mix Claude, Hermes, and Codex private memory surfaces.
Wiki is shared reservoir; runtime directories remain single-writer surfaces.
Prefer JSON unless Markdown is explicitly requested.
Do not store Codex general memory under system_v4/a2_state or any A2 state surface.
Treat source-field lists as provisional scaffolds unless explicitly admitted as canon.
People, schools, and traditions can be added when they improve MMM behavior or sim-routing clarity.
Capture broad thread context when the user identifies forgotten context, not only polished outputs.
Save actual worker wave receipts separately from council summaries.
When the user gives small formatting or behavior feedback, apply the correction and continue the prior task instead of making the whole response about the error.
Preserve full grouped follow-up fields for tuning/plurality work, with compact single-spaced options and full operational All bundles.
Keep Results, Follow-up, Voice Blocks, and LLM Council as separate surfaces.

Every visible Codex output uses the Wizard surface order: Main Answer, Results, Follow-up, Hygiene & Security. This includes progress updates, error corrections, audit summaries, and short acknowledgements.
When durable formatting/process feedback is given, save it into Codex wiki memory before treating it as resolved, then continue the prior task inside the full output format.
When a concrete plan has already been made, execute the promised saved step or runtime step; do not substitute another explanation for completion.
Wizard format means the full operating machinery for the turn, not just four headings: voices/council when relevant, receipt truth, grouped follow-up for tuning/plurality, and hygiene/scope truth.
Every Codex input and output attempts all stable voices and real subagents; report spawned, blocked, deferred, and shutdown truth.
Attempt means a lane registry decision exists; constrained or trivial turns may defer nonrun lanes with explicit reason, but must not fake visible execution.
Systems, Strategy, and Factory are whole-context voices that inspect the whole thread, plan, queue, drift, handoffs, and return-to-sims gate.
Follow-ups run as a three-wave pipeline: make, run, audit/improve.
Current Wizard packet is v4.2 at /Users/joshuaeisenhart/wiki/wizard/packet-v4-2-current; do not silently fall back to v4.1.
Bounded means local claim ceiling and no salient skip-ahead, not low volume; run many independent bounded packets in parallel when safe.
Tool integration and tool-lego fit are main work; cleanup is side-lane only when it blocks tool receipts, runner safety, or evidence truth.
Numpy/scipy are classical baseline tools; numpy-only sims do not prove QIT, GStack, axis, bridge, or nonclassical claims.
Wizard is a routing/audit/MMM/fanout harness, not a substitute for sim/tool receipts.
canonicality: Codex wiki memory manifest, not project A2 state
